topics: Special Function Registers. Addressing modes of 8051. Instruction set of 8051-Assembler directives
compare and rotate instructions- Bit processing instructions- Program flow control instructions
Illustrative examples using of all types of instructions. Programming the ports of 8051. Programming in C
Illustrative examples of C programming for 8051-time delay and I/O operations
programming the timers in assembly language and C language
timer as an event counter. Serial Communication with 8051
RS232 protocol. Serial communication programming in assembly language. Interrupt system of 8051
introduction to interrupts-interrupts in 8051-priority of interrupts- Interrupt handling and execution for timer
external and serial interrupts. Programming to handle all types of interrupts in assembly language
memory organization. Introduction to PIC microcontrollers
Core and peripheral features Project based learning

students shall be given an awareness and/ or guidelines on how to implement learn this course through a project and form groups of maximum 5 members in the first week. • Each group identify a project where microcontrollers can be used for simple biomedical applications and perform a literature survey. • Choose a suitable microcontroller/ microcontroller- based development boards for your application and the input-output devices to be interfaced • Develop the block diagram and present before the jury within first month after the commencement of classes • Implement necessary hardware and software to develop the prototype • Prepare a report and present before the jury for final evaluation
